seo_title: Model Merging on Loss Landscape: A Geometry Perspective Explained
meta_description: A framework for model merging that outperforms baselines in image classification tasks.
body_html:
Model merging is a promising approach for knowledge integration and parallel development without retraining.

Key Benefits of EpiMer

  • EpiMer outperforms baselines in image classification tasks by improving across-task average accuracy and worst-task accuracy.
  • The framework unifies curvature-aware methods and recent spectral methods as special cases of the subspace Fréchet mean with different geometric metrics.
  • EpiMer strictly outperforms the baselines on all three CLIP-ViT backbones at matched rank.

Technical Details

Parameter Description
Fréchet mean A mathematical concept used to solve model merging as a Riemannian manifold.
Low-rank subspace A restricted computation space for EpiMer.

Conclusion

EpiMer provides a framework for model merging that outperforms baselines in image classification tasks, improving across-task average accuracy and worst-task accuracy.

source.title: Model Merging on Loss Landscape: A Geometry Perspective
source.summary: Researchers have proposed a new framework called EpiMer for model merging, which aims to integrate knowledge from multiple models without retraining. The framework uses a geometric perspective to solve the Fréchet mean on a Riemannian manifold, allowing it to efficiently compute the merged model. Through theoretical analysis and experiments on eight image classification tasks, the authors show that EpiMer outperforms existing methods in terms of accuracy, particularly when using curvature-aware merging. The framework also unifies different geometric metrics used in previous methods.
